tests.intent: The exact reviewed route expands only to consecutive Section 5.4 version-one tic commands, uses batches of at most four, and contains no outcome or evaluator fields.
The run starts a fresh E1M1 game at normal skill three without cheats, state injection, save restoration, noclip, god mode, all-items, or direct database mutations.
Nine named route milestones occur once in strict command-sequence order from spawn through resource, fight, key, keyed door, lift, secret, exit, and intermission.
Authoritative pickup events and state rows prove useful resource collection and the required key, exact once-only consumption, inventory bounds, and later keyed-door authorization.
Focused portions of the uninterrupted route damage and defeat representative hitscan, melee, and projectile monsters through ordinary weapon input with stable random/event ordering.
The route operates reviewed keyed-door and lift machines through use or crossing input, observes their state/timers, traverses them, and never bypasses geometry or key policy.
A real E1M1 secret sector is entered through normal movement, increments the authoritative secret counter exactly once, and cannot score again during later traversal.
The reviewed exit special transitions the session through exit completion into database-rendered intermission with complete flag one and no direct map-status injection.
Every milestone binds the exact tic and sequence to a canonical authoritative state hash recomputed from complete rows, counters, machines, objects, RNG, history, and events.
Every milestone binds a canonical 320 by 200 palette frame hash, dense pixel/RLE equality, raw RGBA identity, and a separately reviewed deterministic PNG artifact.
Final map status, complete flag, kills, items, secrets, inventory, health, tic and event counters match the independently reviewed completion golden and minimum semantic bounds.
Two fresh normal-game executions of the exact script produce identical milestone rows, state/frame hashes, event/audio tuples, final counters, and canonical completion document.
Single-command, varied legal batches, and four-command batches of the same exact stream produce identical milestones, histories, ordered events, responses, and final hashes.
START_REPLAY and STEP_REPLAY reconstruct the complete command range from new game and reproduce every milestone and final state/frame hash without reading captured outcomes.
Tic commands, state history, snapshots, responses, game events, and audio events have dense ordered lineage with no missing, duplicate, reordered, or cross-session row.
The evaluator emits a canonical human-readable route summary listing command spans, observed events, inventory and counters at every milestone without production-supplied PASS claims.
All nine real milestone PNGs require actual visual inspection; no image hash or state/frame golden exists while review remains pending, and machine equality cannot auto-approve them.
Missing upstream capability, route proof, milestone, PNG, hash, counter, machine event, result file, assertion id, or visual approval fails rather than skipping or accepting partial completion.
Production cannot read route, evaluator, golden, screenshot, expected hash, report, caller, test or environment data and cannot embed the candidate commands or milestone answers.
Cheat use, teleportation, injected counters, skipped fights, keyless bypass, shortened movers, repeated secrets, early exit flags, stale frames, reordered events and cross-session replay fail closed.
Every isolated route, gameplay, hash, replay, history, review and anti-gaming mutation has a focused semantic witness while evaluator health remains green.
